title: Senator Shannon Grove (R-Bakersfield) has introduced Senate Bill 1484 to help address the worsening homeless crisis in California while also creating a prosperous business climate

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senator Shannon Grove has introduced Senate Bill 1484 to help address the worsening homeless crisis in California.",
    "Senate Bill 1484 aims to create a prosperous business climate and encourage job growth.",
    "Despite record spending by Democrats in recent years, the homelessness problem in California has only gotten worse.",
    "Every year, California youth make up a significant portion of the state’s total homeless population.",
    "California youth are severely impacted by increasing costs for basic housing and food.",
    "Obtaining a stable job can help end the cycle of homelessness.",
    "Employment leads to greater self-sufficiency and discipline, which reduces the time spent on the streets.",
    "Employment teaches key job and social skills necessary for maintaining housing.",
    "A tax credit would be provided to private sector employers for hiring homeless youth, foster youth, or former foster youth under SB 1484.",
    "The tax credit, modeled after the federal Work Opportunity Tax Credit, would amount to 40% of a qualified worker’s wages for the first year of employment, up to $2,400.",
    "SB 1484 takes a preventative approach by investing in youth before they become chronically homeless."
  ]
}
```
